channel.title: Petr Svihlik - blog
channel.description: Blog about all that matters to me - technology, life, leadership, and Developer Relations.

item.title: The Junior Developer Problem Is More Complicated Than You Think
item.description: A nuanced response to arguments about AI replacing junior developers, examining structural challenges and the profession's evolution.

item.title: AI Is Not a Tooling Conversation. It's an Organizational Stress Test.
item.description: Takeaways from the Pragmatic Summit in San Francisco on AI's organizational impact on engineering teams.

item.title: The First 300 Days of My Microsoft Journey
item.description: My friends and former colleagues have been bombarding me with questions about my time at Microsoft. Here's a detailed account.

item.title: A Curated List of Top 10 Android Games Worth Your Time
item.description: No matter whether you're on a train, on a toilet, or on acid you'll enjoy these top 10 Android games.

item.title: Lessons Learned: Migrating from AppVeyor to GitHub Actions
item.description: The third article about GitHub Actions in a row? One must be thinking I am up to something.

item.title: Reporting .NET 5 XUnit Code Coverage in Codecov via GitHub Actions and Coverlet
item.description: You might remember my earlier post where I described how to set up code coverage reporting in AppVeyor. This is the GitHub Actions version.

item.title: Using Environment Protection Rules to Secure Secrets When Building External Forks with pull_request_target
item.description: Building pull requests from forked repositories with GitHub Actions can be a bit tricky when it comes to secrets.

item.title: Avoiding the DevRel ROI Trap with Better Strategic Alignment
item.description: An alternative approach to proving the value of DevRel, not focused on numbers or metrics but on strategic alignment.

item.title: The Little I Learned About Running
item.description: I had never been good at sports (perhaps with an exception of swimming) and yet I fell in love with running.

item.title: OK Google, water the garden!
item.description: Let's face it, my brain is just not compatible with any device that has a segment display or more than one button.

item.title: The Rise of JAMstack
item.description: JAMstack has proven increasingly popular among developers over the past few years. But what exactly is JAMstack, and why should you care?

item.title: 7 Deadly Sins of OKRs
item.description: Are you implementing Objectives and Key Results across your company and don't want your colleagues to feel frustrated after the first couple of few weeks?

item.title: Building A Culture of Relationships That Persist
item.description: What does it take to build a flourishing company culture? And how to recognize one? Real-world examples of good and bad habits, substantial and irrelevant indicators.

item.title: Running .NET Core 3 XUnit Code Coverage in AppVeyor Using OpenCover and Codecov
item.description: I love AppVeyor. It's my go-to CI/CD service for .NET projects. I use it for both open-source and private projects.

item.title: Why I Won't Hire You - 5 Honest Tips to a Better CV
item.description: Recently, I had the immense pleasure to go through several hundreds of CVs. I thought I'd share some of the most common mistakes I've seen.

item.title: How I Changed My Life in Three Steps
item.description: The year 2018 was absolutely groundbreaking to me. I completely reorganized my life and focused on things that truly matter.

item.title: Three Ways to Manage Data in Kentico Using PowerShell
item.description: PowerShell is the leading task automation platform for Windows. Developers and sysadmins alike use it to automate tasks and manage systems.

item.title: Referencing Multiple Versions of the Same Assembly in a Single Application
item.description: We've all been there — our application depends on a version of assembly that's incompatible with the version that's referenced by the NuGet package we've just installed.

item.title: Running Visual Studio Remote Debugger in Domain Environment
item.description: This article describes scenarios in which you need to connect to Visual Studio Remote Debugging Monitor running on a remote machine that is a part of a Windows domain.
